properties.name: Description
properties.value: A participant in the game who controls one or more Player-heroes.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The Player who controls the game world, all Loremaster Characters and Adversaries, frames scenes, and adjudicates rules. Equivalent to a Game Master in other systems.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The group of Player-heroes who adventure together. A Company may be composed of adventurers of any Heroic Culture and Calling.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A single episode focusing on the Company, or part of it, facing a challenge, preparing for one, or recovering from one.

properties.name: Framing
properties.value: The Loremaster frames each scene by describing the location, identifying which Player-heroes and other characters are present, and defining the challenge the Company faces.

properties.name: Player Agency
properties.value: Players have full control over what their Player-heroes do and how they do it. The Loremaster determines how the world — Loremaster Characters, creatures, and the environment — reacts.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: This Skill measures the capacity to evoke respect in onlookers, and determines the impression the Player-heroes make on someone they meet for the first time. Depending on the circumstances and on the player's intentions, it can be used to instil wonderment, admiration, or even fear. Awe arises from a hero's native charisma, but can also be engineered by a dramatic entrance or impressive attire. Successful use of the Awe Skill evokes the chosen effect, while failure can lead to the opposite result. Achieving a superior success means that a hero has won the hearts of the onlookers or cowed them into silent submission.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Athletics is a broad Skill, covering most of the physical activities that a hero might undertake while adventuring, including running, leaping, climbing, and swimming. Heroes with high Athletics Skill scores exhibit a winning combination of physical prowess, grace, and control, generally gained through continuous exercise and daily exertion. A successful Athletics roll produces a satisfactory outcome in the physical activity, while a failed roll might lead to serious harm, depending on the circumstances. A superior result suggests a spectacular achievement.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The Awareness Skill represents a hero's readiness to react and the ability to notice something unexpected, out of the ordinary, or difficult to detect. A high Skill score reflects both keen senses and the experience to understand what is seen or heard — a high level of watchfulness is extremely useful if a Player-hero serves as a look-out for the Company.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Knowing how to hunt is a fundamental skill, shared by most cultures of Middle-earth. A Hunting roll may be required when pursuing a creature through wild areas, or to follow tracks and identify a quarry by its spoor. The Hunting Skill also covers preparing traps and the training and use of hunting dogs or birds. In wilder areas, hunters learn to apply their trade to more dangerous quarries — such as Orcs, Spiders, or Wargs — or else risk becoming the prey.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Hobbits and Men, Elves and Dwarves, even Goblins and maybe Orcs: all creatures of Middle-earth celebrate by playing music and singing songs. Great deeds and grim misfortunes are remembered in verse, and pleasant or comic stories are told to ease spirits and find comfort. The Song Skill is used to recite poems, sing songs, or play instruments suitable to a character's Culture. It can also be used to learn new works or create original compositions.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The Craft Skill doesn't really cover the whole range of abilities of smiths, wrights, and other artisans, but reflects a talent for making or mending things by hand. Craft can be used to repair the wheel of a cart, or construct an improvised raft with wood found on a river shore, or to start a fire on a windy hill. A high Craft score may indicate that the Player-hero was a craftsperson before starting a life of adventure. Some Cultures, such as the Dwarves and Noldorin Elves, revere crafts as the highest expression of creativity.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Player-heroes can use the Enhearten Skill to instil positive feelings in others, urging them to act on the matter at hand. They achieve this mainly through example, charisma, and personal conviction, rather than through the effective use of words (which falls under Persuade). This Skill can be used on individuals, but is particularly effective in influencing crowds. Player-heroes with high Enhearten ratings can be forceful orators, passionate agitators, and well-loved leaders. A successful Enhearten roll awakens a chosen feeling in the subject, as long as it is not in opposition to their current mood. A superior success is enough to influence wholly disinterested individuals, or turn rivals into supporters.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: In the Third Age, the cities, villages, and towns of Middle-earth are often separated by many leagues of wild or deserted areas. Roads that used to lead safely to distant realms now end in broken trails that go nowhere. Whichever way the Company chooses to go, it will be a long and weary way before they get there. A successful roll of Travel may let a Player-hero estimate the length of a journey, read a map, and even evaluate whether a group of strangers on the road can be approached safely or if they should be left alone instead. A good Travel Skill is an invaluable asset for the Guide of the Company when on a journey.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Insight represents the ability to see beyond appearances and to recognise the hidden thoughts and beliefs of people. Insight does not reveal if someone is lying, but the information it yields can be used to draw useful conclusions about people's motives. Heroes with a high Insight score are often recognised as being sensible and discerning individuals, and many may seek their counsel. A successful Insight roll provides the Player-heroes with a reliable, but partial, portrait of the person they are observing. A superior success allows them to correctly guess additional details about an individual's state of mind. Insight tests may be rolled in opposition to an adversary trying to sway or convince a Player-hero of something.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The knowledge of how to relieve pain and apply remedies to restore health to the suffering is an ancient one, and treatments differ from culture to culture. However, almost all traditions agree on the treatment of serious injuries, which must be tended immediately to keep from worsening. The Healing Skill includes bone setting and the use of herbs or salves, but the outcome relies on the ability of the healer to understand what ails the sufferer and determine what should be done to help them.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The Free Peoples recognise common norms of decency and ancient conventions of behaviour. Observing these traditions demonstrates respect and is a way of quickly establishing a friendly footing even with complete strangers. Succeeding in a Courtesy roll might mean the Player-heroes know what to say at the right moment to make a good impression with their host, or are mindful of their manners when receiving guests.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A rating in this Skill shows a firm grasp of the rules of battle and the capability to maneuver appropriately when involved in a violent confrontation. The Battle Skill can be used to gain an advantage when fighting against a group of foes, or leading a company of troops in open battle and maintaining order in the midst of chaos. Individual fighting talents are covered by Combat Proficiencies.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: This Skill allows the Player-heroes to apply their reasoning to convince other individuals of an idea or course of action. It can be used to influence small groups of listeners, but only if used in an appropriate context, such as a common hall. Persuasion requires more time than other Personality Skills, but can have a more lasting impact on other characters' actions. A high Persuade score denotes an uncommon eloquence, a love of speech and knowledge of its proper use and its effect on listeners. Wise people, advisers to chieftains and kings, and their messengers all share this passion for the spoken word. The successful use of the Persuade Skill allows a Player-hero to convince an audience that their current position regarding a matter is flawed. A superior success convinces listeners of the quality of a hero's stance, and may even turn them into ardent believers.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Player-heroes resort to Stealth whenever they need to act in a furtive or secret way. The Skill includes hiding, moving quietly, and shadowing others. These activities often rely on quickness and precision, so a stealthy Player-hero combines practised caution with the ability to judge the right moment to take a chance. Hunters, burglars, and solitary fighters use Stealth to prosper in their trade. A successful Stealth roll indicates that the hero has gone unnoticed, while failure almost certainly attracts unwanted attention. A superior success produces an outcome so flawless that it may even be impossible to notice the deed after the fact.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Player-heroes can use the Scan Skill when examining something closely or attentively. This Skill allows a Player-hero to skim through a book to locate a piece of relevant information, look for concealed doors or hidden inscriptions, recognise a familiar face in a crowd, or locate a set of tracks on the ground. Scan rolls are generally initiated by the players rather than the Loremaster. One roll is required for each inspection of a small area, such as a room. Awareness, rather than Scan, is used to see if a hero passively notices something. A successful Scan roll generally lets Player-heroes find what they are looking for — if it is to be found at all. A superior success usually means the object is found more quickly.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Player-heroes rely on the Explore Skill when they move through an unfamiliar area of the Wild. An Explore roll may be required during a journey to find out where the Company is heading, or to get back on track after a detour; to cope with adverse weather conditions or other natural hazards; to create paths through the wilderness suitable for others to follow; or to choose a suitable place to set up camp. A high Explore rating is an invaluable resource if a Player-hero is acting as a scout for the Company.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Owing its name to the ancient game, the Riddle Skill represents the ability to draw conclusions from seemingly unconnected scraps of information by deduction, reasoning, and intuition. Adventurers also rely on Riddle whenever they are forced to talk about a subject but prefer to conceal part of what they know; for example, to tell something about themselves without revealing their identity. As an accepted custom among many cultures, speaking in riddles is usually allowed among strangers meeting for the first time and wanting to speak guardedly. This Skill is also used to gain helpful insight from a spoken or written riddle.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Lore expresses a love for learning: be it a fascination with descriptions of distant lands or an interest in family genealogy. Whenever an action involves knowledge of some kind, a Lore test is required. Player-heroes are considered to be knowledgeable in the traditions of their own people, and so the Loremaster should rarely require a player to make a Lore roll for information regarding their own character's Culture or the area they originally came from.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: In charge of all decisions concerning route, rest, and supplies. Makes all Marching Tests rolling Travel at TN equal to the Guide's own Heart TN. Only one Guide is allowed per Company.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: In charge of finding food in the wild. Faces journey events using Hunting.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: In charge of keeping watch. Faces journey events using Awareness.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: In charge of setting up camp and opening new trails. Faces journey events using Explore.

properties.name: Cultural Blessing
properties.value: VALOUR rolls are Favoured (roll two Feat Dice, keep best).

properties.name: Cultural Blessing
properties.value: Halve the Load rating of any armour you're wearing (including helms, but not shields), rounding fractions up.

properties.name: Cultural Weakness
properties.value: NAUGRIM: Dwarven adventurers cannot use great bow, great spear, or great shield.

properties.name: Cultural Blessing
properties.value: If you are not Miserable, you can spend 1 point of Hope to achieve a Magical success on a roll when using a Skill in which you possess at least one rank.

properties.name: Cultural Weakness
properties.value: THE LONG DEFEAT: When it is time to remove accumulated Shadow during the Fellowship Phase, you can only remove a maximum of 1 point.

properties.name: Cultural Blessing
properties.value: Your WISDOM rolls are Favoured, and you gain (1d) on all Shadow Tests made to resist the effects of Greed.

properties.name: Cultural Weakness
properties.value: HALFLINGS: Available weapons are axe, bow, club, cudgel, dagger, short sword, short spear, spear. Cannot use a great shield.

properties.name: Cultural Blessing
properties.value: Each Man of Bree in the Company increases the Fellowship Rating by 1 point.

properties.name: Cultural Blessing
properties.value: Each Hobbit of the Bree-land in the Company increases the Fellowship Rating by 1 point.

properties.name: Cultural Weakness
properties.value: HALFLINGS: Available weapons are axe, bow, club, cudgel, dagger, short sword, short spear, spear. Cannot use a great shield.

properties.name: Cultural Blessing
properties.value: Add 1 point to one Attribute of your choice during character creation.

properties.name: Cultural Weakness
properties.value: ALLEGIANCE OF THE DÚNEDAIN: During the Fellowship Phase (except at Yule), you may recover Hope up to a maximum equal to half your HEART score (rounded up).

properties.name: Shadow Path Description
properties.value: When individuals are given a position of authority, either by rank, lineage, or stature, they may end up mistaking their own aggrandisement for the greater good of the people they should be guiding or keeping safe. Power is the quintessential temptation, and provides the Shadow with an easy way to win the hearts of those who desire it.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: When the world is on the brink of ruin, it is the duty of all individuals of worth to rise and take the lead, whatever the risk. You have chosen to put your judgement to the service of others, to guide them in this dark hour. But you don't want others to heed your commands out of fear or obedience — you want them to follow you because they trust you.

properties.name: Shadow Path Description
properties.value: Individuals who live by the sword are ever tempted to draw it, either literally or figuratively, when their will is thwarted or when they deem their honour to have been impugned by an insult. As corruption spreads in their spirit, their behaviour worsens, leading to more extreme violent reactions.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: You deem that there is but one way to oppose the return of the Shadow, and that it is to conquer it by strength of arms. You are recognised as a warrior among your folk, a valiant fighter, onward into battle. For you, the road to adventure leads straight to wherever your foes prowl or hide.

properties.name: Shadow Path Description
properties.value: Travelling afar might be the duty chosen by a messenger, but it carries the risk of never finding a place to fight for. The Road goes ever on and on, it's true, but whither then?

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The Wise hold that evil days lie ahead, and that to keep hope, all who fight the Enemy must be as one. Yet, many miles and centuries of isolation separate the Free Peoples, and estrangement breeds mistrust. You have decided that it is your duty to travel to distant lands, carrying tidings and warning people of the coming danger.

properties.name: Shadow Path Description
properties.value: Inquisitiveness and curiosity are desirable virtues in an individual, but knowledge can be put to malicious use, and learned individuals can look down on others as ignorant fools. Secrets are dangerous, as the very desire of uncovering them may corrupt the heart.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: For you, knowledge makes the wild world a less threatening place. Yellowed maps in lost books replace a fear of the unknown with curiosity and wonder, songs composed in ages past strengthen the weariest of hearts. A love of learning guides your every step, and illuminates the way for you and those who listen to your advice.

properties.name: Shadow Path Description
properties.value: Adventurers who find themselves on the Road to seek lost riches run the risk of catching the age-old disease capable of turning a pile of enchanted gold into bitter ashes. As the Shadow tightens its grip on their hearts, the world shrinks around them and their closely guarded possessions.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The world has seen the passing of the glory of many Dwarven kings and Elven lords, and their heritage is now buried in Orc-infested dungeons. Hoards of stolen gold and jewels, guarded by fell beasts, beckon all who dare to find them. You seek to recover what is lost, even when it means braving unspeakable dangers.

properties.name: Shadow Path Description
properties.value: Self-doubt is often the way that the Shadow chooses to reach the heart of those who oppose it. For they know that the Enemy is strong and terrible and that those they protect are too naive or weak to fend for themselves. Every day they ask themselves: will my strength be enough to prevail, or will I drag down the innocent in defeat?

properties.name: Description
properties.value: In this age of the world, when shadows grow deeper with every passing year, you have sworn to defend all who cannot defend themselves. Often, your choice forces you to forsake civilised areas, to better guard their inhabitants from what lurks right outside their fences. This has made you a stranger in the eyes of the common folk, a threatening figure like those you are protecting them from.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Raise your maximum Hope rating by 2 points.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Overcoming difficulties has reinforced your spirit and renewed your faith in a brighter future.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: When inflicting Special Damage in combat, add +1 to your STRENGTH rating on a Heavy Blow, and +1 to the Feat Die numerical result on a Pierce.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Your blows have become more forceful and your aim steadier, allowing you to do greater harm.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Raise your maximum Endurance rating by 2 points, or by your WISDOM rating, whichever is higher.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Your determination and stamina have improved through hardship and toil.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Choose two Skills and make them Favoured.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: You have practised certain skills until they have become as natural as breathing.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Raise your Parry rating by 1 point.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Your skill (or fortune?) in battle has increased with your growth in wisdom.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Lower one of your Attribute TNs by 1.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Adventuring is honing your inborn talents.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Each time you gain Fatigue from a Journey Event, you gain 1 point less. Additionally, when you take a Short Rest, you and all members of the Company regain an extra amount of lost Endurance equal to your WISDOM rating.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: You have been taught the ancient recipe of cram, a biscuit-ish cake that keeps good indefinitely, made by your folk for long journeys. Albeit certainly not entertaining (and in fact, mostly a chewing exercise), it is doubtlessly sustaining and very nutritious.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: When you are fighting creatures with Might 2 or more, all your attack rolls are Favoured.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The legend of Bard the Bowman has inspired many young men and women from Dale, so much so that they long to prove their worth with the killing of a great monster. As many have before you, you have long pondered on the ways to deal with large creatures, hoping one day to gain renown defeating them.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: If your Fellowship focus is a Dwarf, whoever among you is fighting in a Defensive stance may attempt the Protect Companion combat task to benefit the other as a secondary action. Additionally, Dwarves are always considered to be Friendly towards you during the Interaction stage of a council.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The days of the Dragon have forged a strong alliance between the Bardings and the Dwarves of Erebor.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: When you score a Piercing Blow on a ranged attack, the PROTECTION roll of the target is Ill-favoured.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The Black Arrow that brought down Smaug the Dragon may have been fated to do so, but the hand that sent it flying so fiercely was exceedingly strong. When you throw a spear or bend your bow, you make sure that your grip is steady and your aim true.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Raise your maximum Endurance rating by 1. The first time that you receive a Wound that would normally kill you — a deadly wound — you are saved by some miraculous circumstance that leaves you Wounded but alive, and you immediately raise your maximum Hope rating by 1 point. Then, your destiny is completed (you can do this only once).

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Stories tell that those who belong to the bloodline of Dale are destined for greatness, a fact proven true by your King and his story. Do you share the same destiny of those of the line of Girion?

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: You can communicate with any bird with appropriate Skill rolls, usually using COURTESY, PERSUADE, or SONG, to receive tidings, deliver messages, and so on. Additionally, when you are outside, once during each Combat, Council or Journey, you can choose to become Inspired on any one roll.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Stories told in the North speak of how Dalemen can become so wise as to comprehend the speech of birds, and so by understanding their song be forewarned of impending threats, or be able to spot a weakness in their enemies' defences.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Once per combat, when you fight in a Forward stance you can make your attack roll Favoured and attempt the Intimidate Foe combat task as a secondary action.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The secret tongue of the Dwarves is guarded as a treasure of the past, yet their battle-cry is well-known and feared by their chief foes.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Choose three Skills in which you possess at least one rank and mark them on your character sheet. Whenever you use any one of the three marked Skills, you can spend 1 Hope to achieve a Magical success.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: In ancient times, the Dwarves made mighty spells. You have been taught fragments of old enchantments that retain some power to this day.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: When you are in the dark (at night, or underground) you are Inspired on all your rolls.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Your kind is not troubled by mere darkness in itself, as most of the members of other cultures are. On the contrary, you have grown to like the dark better than the light, finding yourself at ease in its cold embrace.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Add +2 to your Parry rating when fighting underground, or in otherwise cramped quarters, like inside a building.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The Dwarves have fought many wars in deep places beneath the earth. You have been taught how to best defend yourself while fighting underground. As a result, you know how to exploit corners, darkness, and other natural obstacles to your advantage.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Raise your maximum Endurance rating by 1. All your PROTECTION rolls are Favoured, as long as you are not Miserable.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The Dwarves were made to be strong and to endure, for they came unto the world when a great evil ruled Middle-earth. As long as your spirit supports your strength, you can resist any hurt of the body more hardily than most.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Raise your maximum Hope rating by 1 point. You gain (1d) on all Shadow Tests made to resist the effects of Sorcery.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Dwarves were made from their beginning to resist any form of domination. You have strengthened your resolve against all but the most subtle of the weapons of the Enemy.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: All your Shadow Tests due to Dread are Favoured, and you additionally gain (1d) on those rolls forced upon you by an evil spirit or ghost (including creatures possessed by one).

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Elves can perceive creatures that dwell in the wraith-world, be they spirits or ghosts, even when they are normally invisible to the eyes of the living and appear only as a chilling whisper of voices. You have learnt to strengthen your heart against such terrors.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: When using a Bow (not a Great Bow) and fighting in Rearward Stance, you may attempt the Prepare Shot combat task as a secondary action.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Elves possess a natural talent for hitting the mark when using their bows. You have honed this talent almost to perfection, and your arrows find their target with uncanny precision.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Raise your maximum Hope rating by 1 point. During the Adventuring Phase you can become Inspired on a number of rolls equal to your WISDOM rating.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The name of Elbereth, the Queen of the Stars, is revered by all the Eldar still dwelling in Middle-earth. You have resorted to calling on her name in moments of great need, asking the Kindler to bestow her grace upon you.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: You don't need to sleep, as long as you can engage in simple, repetitive activities. When you take a Short Rest you are considered to have had a Prolonged Rest instead.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The spirit of Elves is so strong that their bodies recover swiftly from many ills and injuries. As your WISDOM has grown you have felt less and less the need to lie down to get better. Instead, you can recover from your exertions while awake, finding repose in the secret way of your folk.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: On a successful attack roll your adversary additionally loses one point of Hate or Resolve, plus one point for each Success icon.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Your folk have seen many defeats, and many fruitless victories in their wars against the Shadow. The deadly rage that your kindred harbour for the Enemy infuses your weapons with a gleam of chill flame.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: When you are targeted by a Journey Event while you are in a Wild Land, roll on the Journey Events table as if you were in a Border Land, and as if you were in a Wild Land if you are in a Dark Land. Moreover, in addition to your chosen role you are always allowed to cover the Scout role.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Long centuries and uncounted generations of Men seem but a little while to the Elves. Your memories go back to a time before Eriador became desolate, and your knowledge of the land is returning to you now that you are traveling again outside the boundaries of your realm.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: If a location or a situation offers even the smallest opportunity to hide or sneak silently away, make a STEALTH roll: if you succeed, you simply disappear. You can choose to reveal yourself at any moment by simply stepping out into the open from your hiding place.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Hobbits are said to have little or no magic about them, but the way they can choose to disappear quickly and quietly can be described by others only as supernatural. You have learnt to choose exactly the right moment to turn away from the attention of others, sometimes unconsciously anticipating the need to disappear.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: As long as you are Miserable, Weary, or Wounded, you are Inspired on all your rolls.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: One of the Wise once said that you have to put a Hobbit in a tight place before you find out what is in them. You prefer to avoid finding yourself in one, but you are also sure you would be able to take good care of yourself in the circumstances.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: When you are engaged in close combat with a creature bigger than you (very often!) you add +2 to your Parry rating. Additionally, you can assume a Rearward stance in combat even if there is only one other Company member fighting in a Close Combat stance.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Hobbits are a little people, but their resourcefulness far exceeds their stature. You have learnt how to exploit your small size to your advantage in a fight.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: All your ranged attacks are Favoured. If you attack by throwing a stone, the roll produces a Piercing Blow on a result of the Gandalf rune, with an Injury rating of 12.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Hobbits are nimble and keen-eyed, qualities that make them good archers. You have honed this natural talent to perfection, so much so that you just need to stoop for a stone to find yourself armed with a deadly weapon.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Raise your Company's Fellowship rating by 1 point. Additionally, you may select a second Fellowship Focus.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The Wise say that it is sometimes best to trust to friendship than to great wisdom. This is particularly true for Hobbits, as everyone knows that it is hardly possible to separate them from their friends. You have given your trust to your fellow Company members, and a true friendship has grown from the bond unifying you.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: When you are Wounded and must roll to determine the severity of your injury, you roll two Feat Dice instead of one and choose the better of the two results. In addition, double your STRENGTH score when calculating the number of Endurance points you recover when resting.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: They say that Hobbits are difficult to dismay or kill, and that they recover their health at a prodigious pace if allowed to enjoy some peace and quiet. You have discovered that you recover wonderfully from falls and bruises (at the cost of some considerable pain).

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Raise your maximum Hope rating by 1 point. Additionally, your pony has a Vigour rating of 4 (regardless of your Standard of Living). Your pony never leaves your side. If you are forced to abandon it, you can choose to find the pony where you left it when you return, or to have it find its way back to Bree.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Bree-ponies are very similar to their owners, the Bree-folk — they don't seem to have anything special compared to their peers in other parts of Middle-earth, and yet some of them display uncommon courage and exceptional memory. You have acquired an unusually brave and intelligent beast that follows you everywhere.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Raise your maximum Endurance rating by 1. At the end of each Combat scene, if you are not Wounded or Miserable, you recover a number of Endurance points equal to your HEART or WISDOM score.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Many realms have risen and fallen in the Lone-lands of Eriador since the Elder Days. Only ruins remain to mark their boundaries. Yet, the Bree-men have endured, revealing an unexpected reserve of strength and vigour in the face of adversity.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: When you choose to spend Hope on a roll, you can choose to also gain 1 Shadow to be Inspired for that roll.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: You have lived all your life on the edge of the Wild, but the worst news that has come to Bree in your time concerned only brigands or packs of hungry wolves. You don't know yet what the likes of you can do against the Shadow in the East, but you are sure you will stand against it, no matter the cost.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Raise by 1 the maximum number of Skill rolls that you may attempt in the course of a council. Additionally, the folk encountered are always considered Friendly towards you.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Since you started your adventures, you have realised that your folk's custom of trading with foreigners may help you in your endeavours. Your nice manners and friendly demeanor easily gain the sympathies of those you meet, and your ability to inquire about the doings of strangers, without letting curiosity get the better of you, provide you with many opportunities to learn from those you meet along the way.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: During each Fellowship Phase, you receive a rumour from the Loremaster. In addition, if you are in the Bree-land, you gain (1d) on all INSIGHT and RIDDLE rolls.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: It is indeed true that the affairs of the four villages are the chief concern of the Bree-folk, but the most inquisitive among the inhabitants are always ready to gather what tidings come from distant lands. You have learnt that listening to the strange tales that travellers often talk about can prove to be very precious.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Whenever you regain one or more points of Hope, you recover one additional Hope point (regardless of whether it's the Adventuring or Fellowship Phase).

properties.name: Description
properties.value: You have mastered the art of smoking pipe-weed, and you always carry your pipe with you, as well as a bag of tobacco, for using it grants you patience and clarity of mind.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Raise your maximum Endurance rating by 1 point. If you wear a suit of Leather armour (with no helm) or no armour at all, and carry no shield, you never gain Fatigue during a journey.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: They say that a Ranger with a clear trail to follow can never be weary. You hope the saying will prove true, as it will be your fate to often travel in haste, to seek distant lands on urgent errands or to relentlessly hunt your enemies.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: During an Adventuring Phase, you may invoke your gift of foresight a number of times equal to your WISDOM rating, to bestow it upon yourself or on another member of the Company. This gift can be spent at any moment to reroll all the dice involved in any one roll, whether made by the Player-hero or an adversary targeting them.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The Rangers of the North still possess a measure of the foresight that their ancestors possessed in full. In the Twilight of the Third Age, this gift manifests as a sense of watchfulness, and of fear, arising to warn a Ranger of a danger that has not yet come to pass.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: With the help of the Loremaster, create either a Marvellous Artefact, or a Famous Weapon possessing a single Enchanted Reward and up to 2 normal Rewards.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The days of your folk have darkened, and not many of you remain; but ever the memory of the Kings is passed from generation to generation. An artefact going back to the days of Lost Arnor has been in possession of your family in line unbroken. Now you have been deemed worthy to become its keeper.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Once per combat, when you fight in an Open stance you may attempt the Rally Comrades combat task as a secondary action. Additionally, all members of the Company (you included) are Inspired on all their rolls in the following round.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Your kin belongs to a bloodline of kings, but they have learnt to hide from their enemies in the twilight years of their kingdom. Should you choose to reveal your heritage, your noble bearing will cause your allies to cast away their doubts. You reveal yourself by shouting an ancestral battle-cry, or unsheathing a weapon of high lineage, or by showing a device or coat of arms.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: You gain (1d) on all Shadow Tests made to resist the effects of Dread.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: You have been raised in a land dotted with desolate ruins and burial mounds. You have heard the dead whisper with cold voices out of lonely barrows, while ghostly lights dance on haunted hills. When your will strengthens your heart, there is hardly anything in this world that can dismay you.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Whenever you are making a roll using EXPLORE, HUNTING, or TRAVEL, you can spend 1 Hope to achieve a Magical success. Additionally, you are always allowed to cover more than one role during a journey.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The Rangers of the North roam ceaselessly, hunting for wicked things and guarding all frontiers. For you the Wild has become as familiar as the road that leads home is familiar to Hobbits, and the land itself may reveal useful tidings.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: You seek to exploit any opportunity to attack, to the point of exposing yourself to the retaliation of your enemies. Your attack rolls gain (1d). All close combat attacks aimed at you gain (1d).

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: You fight without sparing yourself, but giving proper attention to your enemies' actions. No advantage or disadvantage.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: You fight conservatively, trying to protect yourself or others and holding your ground. All close combat attacks aimed at you lose (1d). Your attack rolls lose (1d) for each opponent engaging you.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: You stay away from the press of the fighting to attack your foes from a distance. You can attack your adversaries using only ranged weapons, and you can only be targeted by attackers using similar weapons.

properties.name: Requirements
properties.value: Player-heroes are allowed to assume a Rearward stance only if the total number of enemies isn't more than twice the number of adventurers in the Company. Furthermore, for each Player-hero in Rearward there must be two other adventurers fighting in Close Combat stances.

properties.name: Notes
properties.value: Includes throwing stones. Cannot cause a Piercing Blow.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: One-handed blades have a range of uses, from skinning animals to settling disputes among brutes. Daggers and knives are very common, and in the wild areas of the land, no-one is found without one in their belt.

properties.name: Notes
properties.value: Can trigger Pierce as if it was a Sword.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Large daggers and knives, or smaller swords created for close-quarters combat.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A straight-bladed, two-edged sword, wielded in one hand to hew or thrust. This is the most common type of sword.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Only superior smiths can produce longer blades. These wonderful Elven and Dwarven weapons, and the keen blades forged from strange metals by the Men of the West, are often known as long swords. A long sword may either be wielded with one hand, or used to hack and sweep with two hands.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A fighting variation on the common woodcutting tool, axes hang from the belt of many adventurers raised in or near forests.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Borne with one or two hands, a long-hafted axe is designed to hack through the toughest of armour. It is difficult to manoeuvre but when mastered it is a fearful weapon. A skilled fighter learns to fight with the long haft as well as the heavy axe head.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The great axe is an impressive weapon that can only be wielded with two hands. Its heavy head can leave both a deep dint on armour as a club and cleave it as a sword.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A heavy digging implement sporting a curved head with a point on one side and a spade-like blade on the other. It was used to fearsome effect by the Dwarves who followed Dain Ironfoot during the Battle of Five Armies.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Approximately six feet in length, a spear can be hurled as a javelin, or deftly thrust with one hand.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Maybe the most versatile weapon of all, a spear can be thrown, or used with one or two hands.

properties.name: Notes
properties.value: Injury 16 when used two-handed. Can throw.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: With a shaft longer than any other spear, a great spear cannot be used as a ranged weapon, and must be wielded with two hands.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The simple bow is not very different from a hunting-bow. It never measures more than five feet in length, so as to be strung more quickly.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: As tall as a Man and offering superior potency, a great bow can only be used by warriors with the height and stature to bend it fully. An arrow from a great bow can pierce the toughest of armour.

properties.name: Notes
properties.value: Ranged weapon. Cannot be used by Dwarves or Hobbits.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A cudgel is any bashing implement used with one hand, like a small club or short staff.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A club is probably the simplest form of weapon. It may consist of a crude, heavy piece of wood, a nasty instrument reinforced with iron, or an elaborately-carved staff used also as a symbol of status.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The simplest suit of armour available, leather armour is made of layers of cured and hardened animal hide sewn together. It is ideal for hunting or travelling as it is lightweight and comfortable, especially compared to mail armour. A leather shirt protects the back, chest, and abdomen.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A close-fitting corslet with long sleeves, extending the protection of leather armour to the wearer's hips.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Mail is the most effective type of armour encountered in Middle-earth at the end of the Third Age. These suits of close-fitting metal rings are created to protect from cutting and thrusting weapons. A mail shirt is a chain garment protecting the back, chest, and abdomen of its wearer.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A coat of mail is a mail shirt with long sleeves. From the shining hauberks of Elven lords to the black mail of Orc-chieftains, mail armour appears in widely different qualities. Ancient coats of Dwarf-make, when found, are matchless and prized possessions.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Worn in battle or for ceremonial purposes, helms are usually made of leather or iron, but sometimes of more precious metals. The shape of a helm or its decoration is often distinctive, as it helps identify the wearer or their folk. More often than not, the protective features of the helm, especially nose-guards or close-fitting cheek-guards, obscure the face and make it impossible to recognise the wearer otherwise.

properties.name: Notes
properties.value: May be removed in combat to lower Load.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Circular and made of wood reinforced by a protruding metal boss, bucklers are usually smaller and lighter than regular shields.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Round or oval, shields are made of several layers of wood, often reinforced by a large central iron boss, usually decorated and engraved. A regular shield offers good protection from arrows, and is very effective at close quarters.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Kite-shaped, round or barrel-shaped, these shields are carried by the sturdiest of warriors, and are used to carry back their bodies should they fall, but are considered too cumbersome and unwieldy by some.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: When making a PROTECTION roll, add +2 to the result.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A suit of Close-fitting armour or a helm has been expertly tailored or altered to fit its wearer, and is thus easier to wear and to move about in.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Reduce the Load rating of the selected item by 2 (to a minimum of 0 Load).

properties.name: Description
properties.value: An item of Cunning Make has been fashioned using superior materials and skills, making it lighter and more flexible.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Add 2 to the Injury rating of the selected weapon. A weapon that can be wielded with one or two hands gets the bonus to both its Injury ratings.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A Fell weapon has been forged to inflict the deepest wounds, and its Piercing Blows are stopped less easily by armour.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Add 1 to the Damage rating of the selected weapon.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A Grievous weapon is strong and heavy, and inflicts terrible harm on its targets.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Attack rolls made with a Keen weapon score a Piercing Blow also on a result of 9 on the Feat Die.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A Keen weapon has been ground to a fine edge and always finds the weakest point when it strikes an adversary.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Add 1 to the shield's Parry bonus.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A Reinforced shield is bound by iron and will endure hard blows.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: When reduced to half Endurance, makes a Shadow Test or flees.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: On a Seize result, heroes must pass a Strength test or be automatically wounded.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: When first reduced to 0 Endurance, roll the Feat Die: on 1–5, the adversary is not defeated yet and keeps fighting at Endurance 1.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: A wound from this weapon is poisoned. The hero continues to lose 1 Endurance per round until healed.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: All hero PROTECTION rolls against this creature are Ill-favoured.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Heroes must pass a Shadow Test at the start of the first round or be Ill-favoured on all actions that round.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: In darkness, this creature's attack rolls are Favoured.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: If a hero exposes this creature to flame, it retreats and makes all rolls Ill-favoured until the flame is extinguished.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: In direct sunlight, this creature's rolls are Ill-favoured.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: This adversary always acts first in the combat round, before any hero.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Normal weapons cannot destroy this creature. Only Magical weapons, fire, or specific weaknesses can reduce it to 0 Endurance permanently.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: This creature cannot be Intimidated or frightened; it ignores the effects of Enhearten and similar abilities.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: At the start of every round, all heroes must make a Shadow Test or act Ill-favoured.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: This creature can move from Rearward to Close range in a single action, bypassing normal movement rules.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Extinguishes all non-magical light sources within range at the start of combat.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Once per round, may target one hero with a curse effect as a secondary action (Loremaster determines effect based on Shadow Path).

entities.name: The Diamond Studs of Old Took

properties.name: Name
properties.value: The Diamond Studs of Old Took

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A pair of flawless diamond studs said to have belonged to the Old Took of the Shire himself. Worn, they lend an air of quiet authority and ancient grace.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A magical golden harp with silver strings that never goes out of tune. Its music carries a peculiar power to move the hearts of listeners.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A belt of pearls and polished crystals crafted to ease movement; the wearer feels as if walking through water parted before them.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The Heart of the Mountain — a great white gem of a thousand facets, found at the roots of the mountain by Thrain I. It shines with its own inner light. Kings have died for it; wars have been fought over it. Its beauty is a kind of accusation.

entities.name: The Great Golden Cup of Thror

properties.name: Name
properties.value: The Great Golden Cup of Thror

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A great two-handed cup of gold, carved with birds and flowers in the fashion of ancient Dwarven craftwork. Wine drunk from it shows the essence of things — what is hidden becomes visible to the drinker's inner eye.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The ceremonial regalia of Erebor's line: a gold-plated ring coat and a silver-hafted axe with a scarlet gemstone set in the grip. When worn together in the presence of Dwarves, the bearer commands instant respect.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A sword of ancient Gondolin make with a jewelled hilt, called Goblin-cleaver in the Common Tongue and Biter by goblins with good reason. It glows with cold blue light in the presence of Orcs.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: An Elven dagger of Gondolin make, dagger-sized in the hands of a man but a proper short sword for a Hobbit. The blade glows cold blue in the presence of Orcs. Named by Bilbo for the sting of a bee — a small name for a weapon that has felled great enemies.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Wrought for a young Elf-prince in ages past, this coat of rings of Mithril weighs no more than a parchment. It can turn blows that would fell a warrior in plate. Given by Thorin to Bilbo — it saved his life when no one knew it.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The mate to Orcrist, called Foe-hammer in Gondolin and Beater by Goblins. A rune-carved blade that gleams in the dark near Orc-sign. Borne by Gandalf, who found it in a Troll-hoard.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Gifted once to a son of Girion of Dale, this silver coat is bright as the moon but strong as triple steel. It has passed through many hands in the long years since Dale fell.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: An ivory horn carved from the tusk of a great northern beast, fashioned for Arveleg, King of Arnor. Its call carries farther than it should, and it stirs the hearts of those who hear it.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A casket of glass and bronze holding a crystal shard from an Elven lamp. It cannot be extinguished by wind or water, and its light reveals hidden paths.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Ancient boots sewn with Elven craft that relieve the wearer of travel fatigue. The roads seem to welcome the feet of the one who wears them.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A Sindarin quiver decorated with scenes of the Vala Oromë. The arrows within never go wide when the hunter's intent is true.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Said to be the whetstone used by the Kings of Durin's Folk to keep their tools sharp. Work done with a blade honed on this stone comes out finer than craft alone should allow.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A wool fabric infused with the essence of courtesy itself. When worn, others find themselves inclined to treat the wearer with respect regardless of circumstance.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A silver ring of little fame — once the work of an apprentice of Celebrimbor, now forgotten. It grants the wearer a subtle attunement to hidden truths and lore.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A seed from a White Tree preserved in a necklace of amber. It carries the memory of Númenor and the healing arts of that lost kingdom.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A blue sapphire cut in the shape of a water drop, said to be the frozen tear of the Elf-maid Nimrodel. It sharpens the senses and brightens the spirit.

entities.name: Twin Armbands of Day and Night

properties.name: Name
properties.value: Twin Armbands of Day and Night

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Forged in Nogrod: the right arm bears a blazing sun, the left a crescent moon. Together they grant extraordinary physical agility and the ability to move without sound.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A golden circlet worn by the rulers of Fornost Erain, Númenórean kings of Arnor. It carries the weight of that lost kingdom's authority.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A Noldor brooch shaped like an eagle's head, celebrating the ancient alliance between the Elves and the Lord of Eagles. It stirs courage in battle and sharpens the eye for opportunity.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A damasked blade whose hilt is engraved with scenes of sea-storms. When swung with force, those near the strike feel the blast of a great wind.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: An open helm made in Nogrod for a warrior princess, engraved with runes. Blows struck while wearing it resound with terrible force.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The tip of a spear, black with wolf-blood, fitted to a short-sword hilt by Dwarven smiths. Against wolves it is singularly lethal.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: White as sea-foam and sewn with silver wires, this corslet was made by seafaring Elves for a chosen protector. It is light as hope and strong as grief.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A beech round-shield reinforced with a dark steel rim and bearing a blacksmith's hammer grip. It fits its bearer perfectly, as if grown there.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The bow with which Angnir ended the suffering of Lord Hadirion. The weapon carries a Shadow Taint from the deed — its arrows fly true, but the bow marks its bearer in some imperceptible way.

properties.name: Additional Advantage
properties.value: Spend Fellowship to make a combat roll Favoured.

properties.name: Agenda
properties.value: Reclaim lost strongholds, eliminate enemy lieutenants, etc.

properties.name: Additional Advantage
properties.value: Raise Fellowship by +1 when choosing the Meet Patron undertaking to visit Bilbo.

properties.name: Agenda
properties.value: Recover lost lore and lost things.

properties.name: Additional Advantage
properties.value: Spend Fellowship to roll again all dice in a roll. Choose the Meet Patron undertaking to receive a rumour.

properties.name: Agenda
properties.value: Rekindle hope, preserve the lore of the Ages.

properties.name: Additional Advantage
properties.value: Spend Fellowship to make a Shadow Test Favoured.

properties.name: Agenda
properties.value: Warn the Free Peoples, inspire them to action.

properties.name: Additional Advantage
properties.value: Resolve Journey Events within the old realm of Arnor as if in a Border Land. Choose Meet Patron undertaking to receive a rumour.

properties.name: Agenda
properties.value: Fight the Enemy, defend the weak.

properties.name: Additional Advantage
properties.value: Spend all your Fellowship to call Tom or Goldberry's intervention anywhere in Tom's country.

properties.name: Agenda
properties.value: Protect the land, find and preserve what was buried.

properties.name: Cultural Blessing
properties.value: When you are Wounded, all your Attack rolls and PROTECTION rolls are Favoured.

nested.name: Bynames
nested.value: ["...from or of a specific place", "the Bald", "the Black", "the Bold", "the Captain", "the Cloaked", "the Crooked", "the Eloquent", "the Foresighted", "the Good", "the Good-sword", "the Loyal", "the Old", "the Pugnacious", "the Quick-witted", "the Quiet", "the Red", "of the Red-shield", "the Rich", "the Runner", "the Sad", "the Sharp", "the Smith", "the Thin", "the Trouble-maker", "the Wise", "the Young"]

properties.name: Cultural Blessing
properties.value: When you are in a forest, or at night, you can spend 1 point of Hope to achieve a Magical success on a skill roll.

properties.name: Cultural Blessing
properties.value: Add +2 to your Parry rating when fighting in a forest.

nested.name: Nicknames
nested.value: ["the Bird-keeper", "the Bowman", "the Bride", "the Bright one", "the Eagle", "the Healer", "the Hound", "the Hunter", "the Quick", "the Shepherd", "the Shield-bearer", "the Silent one", "the Spear-shaker", "the Wood-goer"]

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Choose three STRENGTH Skills and mark them. Whenever you use any one of the three marked Skills, you can spend 1 Hope to achieve a Magical success.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Raise your maximum Endurance by 1 point. You don't lose (1d) on your Brawling attacks, and your unarmed attack rolls produce a Piercing Blow on a result of a Gandalf rune, with an Injury rating of 12. Additionally, you can communicate with bears by making appropriate Skill rolls, usually using COURTESY, PERSUADE, or SONG, to receive tidings, deliver messages, and so on.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: When inflicting Special Damage in combat, add +2 to your STRENGTH rating on a Heavy Blow.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: If you wear a suit of Leather armour or no armour at all, you gain (1d) on your PROTECTION rolls.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: When you score a Piercing Blow on a close combat attack, the PROTECTION roll of the target is Ill-favoured.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Each time you gain Fatigue from a Journey Event, you gain 1 point less. Additionally, raise your Company's Fellowship rating by 1 point.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Spend 1 point of Hope to light a torch or lamp with magical flame. The Loremaster must choose a group of creatures among those observing the light whose total MIGHT score does not exceed your WISDOM rating — the selected creatures must draw near to the flame. You can control the light at will. If attacked, enemies standing by the light are surprised, won't be able to make opening volleys nor take actions in the first Close Quarters Round, and are temporarily confused and fight as if Weary for the first two rounds of combat.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: When you are in a forest, or at night, you are Inspired on all your combat rolls.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: If you are not Wounded or Miserable, you can spend 1 Hope to recover a number of Endurance points equal to your HEART or VALOUR score, whichever is higher.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: You gain (1d) on all your HEALING rolls. Additionally, at the end of a journey, you can roll HEALING to reduce your accumulated Fatigue (in addition to rolling TRAVEL to do so).

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Your opening volleys and close combat attacks made during the first combat round of a battle are Favoured.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: You have chosen a wolfhound of Wilderland to accompany you. When you make an AWARENESS or HUNTING roll you gain (1d) if the hound is with you. In combat, the hound counts as another Player-hero fighting in a Close Combat stance, and the first close combat attack aimed at you each round loses (1d). If you are hit by a Piercing Blow, you can choose to cancel it, wounding your hound instead. A wounded hound is out of combat for the remainder of a scene and returns at the start of the next session if you succeed in a HEALING roll. On a failure, the hound won't recover until the beginning of the next Adventuring Phase.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Whenever you are making a roll using AWARENESS, EXPLORE, or SCAN, you can spend 1 Hope to achieve a Magical success. Also, you are allowed to cover the Look-out role in addition to any other role during a journey.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Raise your maximum Hope rating by 1 point. Additionally, you can roll SONG to administer First Aid or to save Dying heroes (in addition to rolling HEALING to do so).

properties.name: Cultural Blessing
properties.value: Add 1 point to one Attribute of your choice. Additionally, if you are not Miserable, you can spend 1 point of Hope to achieve a Magical success on a skill roll.

properties.name: Cultural Weakness
properties.value: BESET BY WOE: You can remove accumulated Shadow points exclusively during a Yule Fellowship Phase.

properties.name: Cultural Blessing
properties.value: When you are in a forest, or when using a Skill associated with a useful item, you can spend 1 point of Hope to achieve a Magical success. In addition, you start the game with 1 more useful item.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Next Yule, you may either add a single Enchanted Reward of Elven craftsmanship to a weapon of your choice, or create a Marvellous Artefact (your choice). In addition, during any Fellowship Phase you can make a CRAFT or LORE roll. If you succeed, you learn all there is to be discovered about the qualities of a single Marvellous Artefact or Wondrous Item in the Company's possession.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Raise your maximum Hope score by 1 point. If you are the spokesperson during a council, you raise its time limit by 1, OR you can choose to make all Mortals that were present during a council retain only a vague memory of what was said or agreed. This counts as a lesser magical effect for the purposes of increasing Eye Awareness.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: When an adversary spends a point of Hate or Resolve to activate a Fell Ability, you can spend a point of Hope to cancel its effects.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: When you roll a 10 or a Gandalf rune on the Feat die, your result counts as a Magical success without needing to spend Hope.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Each time you gain Fatigue from a Journey Event, you gain 1 point less. Additionally, you and all members of the Company can get rid of Fatigue on every Prolonged Rest, not only when in a safe and sheltered refuge, and at a rate of 2 points of Fatigue for each Prolonged Rest.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Raise your maximum Hope score by 1 point. Additionally, during each Fellowship Phase, you may either return to Lórien or gain 1 Shadow point to recover twice the usual number of Hope points.

properties.name: Notes
properties.value: A Great Bow, but counts as a Bow for the purpose of Deadly Archery. Gain (1d) on SCAN rolls when attempting the Prepare Shot combat task. You can spend 1 Hope for a Magical success on such rolls.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Choose a second Calling for your character, gaining two additional Favoured Skills, its Distinctive Feature, and its Shadow Path. Start with 3 Virtues (one must be Common) and 3 Rewards. Choose two heirlooms from: a Famous Weapon/Armour with one Enchanted Reward and up to 2 normal Rewards; a Wondrous Item; a Marvellous Artefact and a Precious Object worth 30 Treasure; or a Precious Object worth 90 Treasure.

properties.name: Cultural Blessing
properties.value: Halve the Load rating of any armour you're wearing (rounding fractions up), including helms (but not shields).

properties.name: Cultural Weakness
properties.value: PETTY-DWARVES: Cannot use great bow, great spear, or great shield. You can only choose another Dwarf as your Fellowship Focus.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Raise your maximum Endurance rating by 1 point. Any Endurance loss from extreme cold, fire, and poison you suffer is reduced by one level (grievous to severe, severe to moderate, moderate to none).

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Next Yule, you may either add a single Enchanted Reward of Dwarven craftsmanship to a mail armour or close combat weapon of your choice, or create a Marvellous Artefact (your choice).

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Reclaimers are driven by the desire to restore what was lost and reclaim the glories of the past. They are scholars, artisans, and seekers of ancient knowledge.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Pathfinders are scouts and explorers, always seeking the next passage, the hidden way, the forgotten road through the darkness.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Standard-bearers are leaders and inspirers, keeping hope alive among their companions through stirring words and deeds.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Guardians are watchful protectors, vigilant against threats both physical and spiritual, tending to the well-being of their companions.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Vanguards are warriors and tactical leaders, first into the breach and last to retreat.

properties.name: Fellowship Rating
properties.value: 3 + bonuses from Virtues, Cultural Blessings, Patron

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Solo play in Moria uses Shared Callings instead of standard Callings. Previous Experience is 15 AP. Fellowship Rating is 3 plus any bonuses. Maximum Hope is increased by 5 (representing Band support). Balin is the default Patron (Fellowship bonus +1, Balin's Counsel: spend Fellowship to make combat rolls Favoured).

properties.name: Source
properties.value: Moria — Through the Doors of Durin

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The Band represents a group of Dwarf Allies accompanying the Player-hero. Allies are managed through Disposition rolls (Feat Die + Success Dice equal to Disposition rating) against the Band's Readiness TN. Each Ally has a unique Gift and Quirk. Allies can be distinguished as Hardened through acts of bravery. The Band becomes Weary when half or more mission Allies are lost or suffering serious conditions.

properties.name: Source
properties.value: Moria — Through the Doors of Durin

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: When targeted by an attack, spend 1 Hate to make the attack roll *Ill-favoured*.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Spend 1 Hate to restore 1 Hate to all allies in the fight.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Spend 1 Hate to *gain (2d)* on a Protection roll.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Wounded targets make an *Ill-favoured* Feat die roll to determine the severity of their Injury.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: The Balrog is immune to Endurance loss and Wounds from all sources of injury except magical weapons, and can spend 1 Hate to cancel a Wound from such weapons (unless they have been wrought for the Bane of the Enemy itself). When an attack inflicts damage to the Balrog that would cause it to go to zero Endurance, it causes a Piercing Blow instead. Then, if the Balrog is still alive, its Endurance score is set back at half its maximum rating.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: When a Player-hero hits the Balrog with a close combat attack, the Player-hero suffers severe Endurance loss from fire damage. The Balrog cannot use this ability when immersed in water.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: The creature can attack any Player-hero, in any combat stance, including Rearward.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: All attack rolls against the creature are *Ill-favoured*.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: If an attack results in a Wound, the target is also poisoned (see page 134 of *The One Ring*).

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: When a nearby Player-hero fails any roll, spend 1 Hate to make them gain 2 Shadow points (Sorcery).

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Spend 1 Hate to raise the Eye Awareness of the Company by 1. If the total Eye Awareness is equal to or greater than the Hunt Threshold, there is an immediate Revelation episode.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Spend 1 Hate to inflict a moderate Endurance loss from Poison to all Player-heroes in close combat stance.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Player-heroes can attempt a special combat task against Uftak while in Forward stance. The acting player makes a **RIDDLE** roll as the main action for the round: on a success, Uftak loses 1 point of Hate, plus 1 point for each Success icon rolled.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Har’s Might is considered 1 higher for the purpose of resisting the Intimidate Foe combat task.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: When targeting the object of his hatred, all attacks are *Favoured*.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Spend 1 Hate to cancel a success (not a Magical success) on any Skill roll made by a nearby Player-hero. The roll becomes a failure instead.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Spend 1 Hate to cancel a Wound. When an attack inflicts damage to the creature that would cause it to go to zero Endurance, it causes a Piercing Blow instead. Then, if the creature is still alive, it returns to full Endurance. This ability is ineffective against Player-heroes wielding any magical weapon.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: This creature is invisible, and **AWARENESS**, **SCAN**, and attack rolls against it *lose (2d)*, while the creature’s attack rolls are *Favoured* and *gain (1d)* against Player-heroes who cannot see it. A Player-hero who obtains a Magical success on an **AWARENESS** or **SCAN** roll can see the creature normally and ignores all penalties for the rest of the fight.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Spend 1 Resolve to cancel a Wound. When an attack inflicts damage to the creature that would cause it to go to zero Endurance, spend 1 Resolve to bring the creature back to full Endurance again.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: When a close combat weapon scores a Piercing Blow against the Stone Ghost, spend 1 Resolve to break that weapon. This ability does not affect weapons with one or more Rewards (enchanted or otherwise).

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Spend 1 Hate to attack any Player-hero, in any combat stance, including rearward (same as *Great Leap*).

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: The Watcher in the Water makes one additional attack each round. Each time it receives a Wound, however, for the rest of the fight the Watcher in the Water can make one less attack.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Spend 1 Hate to make all Player-heroes who can hear the creature gain 2 Shadow points (Dread). Those who fail their Shadow test are daunted and cannot spend Hope for the rest of the fight.

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A disc of green glass, engraved with the image of a stag standing on a hilltop. Footprints seen through the disc glow faintly. Made as a gift for the Elves of Eregion, and especially potent in lands that remember them.

properties.name: Source
properties.value: Moria — Through the Doors of Durin

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A ring of gold, set with a large red stone. Those who speak with the wearer feel as though they are relaxing by a fireside with merry company, no matter the circumstances.

properties.name: Source
properties.value: Moria — Through the Doors of Durin

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A silver necklace of such surpassing craftsmanship and delicacy that it inspires those who study it. Often, they share the dreams or memories of great smiths who lived in ages past.

properties.name: Source
properties.value: Moria — Through the Doors of Durin

properties.name: Description
properties.value: The cast head of a Dwarven scholar, with vocal cords of Mithril and eyes of polished quartz. Under moonlight, it whispers secrets. Said to have been forged by the mad Dwarf-scholar Dero, who descended into unknown tunnels beneath the world and returned with strange tales and secrets.

properties.name: Source
properties.value: Moria — Through the Doors of Durin

properties.name: Description
properties.value: An ornate sheath for a sword, made of polished black leather and brass, decorated with images of storm-clouds and lightning bolts. When a weapon is drawn from it, thunder rolls. Made for a King of the North-men in the Second Age of the world.

properties.name: Source
properties.value: Moria — Through the Doors of Durin

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Made from a Dragon's horn, bound in steel, and marked with runes of power, this horn has not been sounded since the elder days. It also carries a curse — if blown, whoever hastens to aid the bearer is doomed, and their Protection rolls in battle are Ill-favoured.

properties.name: Source
properties.value: Moria — Through the Doors of Durin

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A black staff bound in mithril that grants these blessings in addition to any other properties (described in more detail on page 134).

properties.name: Source
properties.value: Moria — Through the Doors of Durin

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A harp associated with the Dimrill Stair and a maiden who dwelt there long ago, in love with a shepherd who perished in the winter snows. She sang a lament until her fingers bled and her heart froze, and she too perished. Despite this sad tale, the harp bears no curse.

properties.name: Source
properties.value: Moria — Through the Doors of Durin

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Made for Dwarven messengers in the days of many Dwarven kingdoms. Heavy boots with soles of iron, yet marvellously light and comfortable.

properties.name: Source
properties.value: Moria — Through the Doors of Durin

properties.name: Description
properties.value: A circlet of Mithril studded with pearls, marked with runes of law and judgement.

properties.name: Source
properties.value: Moria — Through the Doors of Durin

properties.name: Fellowship Rating
properties.value: 3 + bonuses from Virtues, Cultural Blessings, and Patron

properties.name: Fellowship Focus
properties.value: None (solo characters do not have a Fellowship Focus)

properties.name: Virtue Advisory
properties.value: Watch for Company-specific benefits which will not apply to solo play (e.g. Three is Company is not recommended).

properties.name: Description
properties.value: Solo Player-heroes use 15 Previous Experience points instead of 10. Fellowship score equals 3 plus any bonuses. No Fellowship Focus. Gains an extra Distinctive Feature: Strider.

properties.name: Effect
properties.value: Wandering the wilds of Middle-earth alone hardens the spirit and sharpens the senses. While journeying, the Player-hero is considered Inspired on all skill rolls.

                "Investigate reports of Orcs gathering in the Misty Mountains.",

                "Investigate rumours of a dragon in the Withered Heath.",
                "Escort a delegation of Dwarves to a meeting with the Elvenking."
            ]
